mission.title: argo: independent version controlled helm values file
mission.description: independent version controlled helm values file. This issue affects 101+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo deploy symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: independent version controlled helm values file

steps.title: Check current Role configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get role -A
kubectl describe role <name> -n <namespace>
```
followup on #2719 reopening this proposal. The author closed it but I disagree with the conclusion and believe this should still be pursued.

steps.title: Apply the fix for independent version controlled helm values file
steps.description:
# Feature: External Helm values from git

This PR allows for external values.yaml from other git repos in a helm installation. 

**Sample application.yaml**

```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: external-test
  finalizers:
    - resources-finalizer.argocd.argo
```yaml
...
  source:
    repoURL: https://kubernetes-charts.storage.googleapis.com
    chart: my-chart
    targetRevision: 1.0.0
    helm:
      valuesRepo: https://gitlab.com/someproject/path.git
      valuesFiles:
        - values/some/value.yaml
```

steps.title: Confirm independent version controlled helm values file is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: # Feature: External Helm values from git

This PR allows for external values.yaml from other git repos in a helm installation. 

**Sample application.yaml**

```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: external-test
  finalizers:
    - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
    targe. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
...
  source:
    repoURL: https://kubernetes-charts.storage.googleapis.com
    chart: my-chart
    targetRevision: 1.0.0
    helm:
      valuesRepo: https://gitlab.com/someproject/path.git
      valuesFiles:
        - values/some/value.yaml

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: external-test
  finalizers:
    - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
spec:
  project: default
  source:
    repoURL: https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
    targetRevision: 8.5.8
    helm:
      valueFiles:
        - values.yaml
      externalValueFiles:
        - repoURL: https://github.com/KaiReichart/argo-test-values.git
          targetRevision: main
          valueFiles:
            - values.yaml
    chart: mysql
  destination:
    server: 'https://kubernetes.default.svc'
    namespace: default

apiVersion: v2
name: cert-manager
version: 1.1.0
type: application
dependencies:
  - name: cert-manager
    version: v1.1.0
    repository: "https://charts.jetstack.io"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
